Off Calendar Nov-01-2022 Continued To Jan-25-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Erica Martinez Wasdin For Leave To Intervene
Off Calendar Nov-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEDOCONFERENCE):
Atty. AUSTIN SMITH, Esq. of Arias Sanguinetti Wang & Torrijos, LLP (Tel: 510-629-4877) for Intervenor Erica Martinez Wasdin;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: INTERVENOR ERICA WASDIN's Motion For Leave To Intervene.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Movant Erica Wasdin's motion for leave to intervene is granted.

"The court shall, upon timely application, permit a nonparty to intervene in the action or proceeding if either of the following conditions is satisfied: The person seeking intervention claims an interest relating to the property or transaction that is the subject of the action and that person is so situated that the disposition of the action may impair or impede that person's ability to protect that interest, unless that person's interest is adequately represented by one or more of the existing parties. (CCP 387(d)(1)(B))."

Here, Erica Wasdin is entitled to intervene as a matter of right because she meets the statutory requirements set forth in sec. 387(d)(1)(B). The only dispute between the parties is whether Wasdin's interests are adequately represented by Plaintiff. (Opp., 2:20-22.) Wasdin argues that the parties' interests are conflicting because of Plaintiff's understandable desire to maximize her son's recovery, her lack of knowledge about Wasdin's needs, their lack of relationship and communication, and other discovery concerns. (Reply, 4:14-25.) Wasdin's damages must be proven and due to the parties' lack of communication and ability to agree, it is clear that Plaintiff cannot adequately represent her interest.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Intervene
Off Calendar Oct-06-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
INTERVENOR ERICA WASDIN'S Motion For Leave To Intervene:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Mike Danko, Esq. (650-453-3600; mdanko@dankolaw.com) of Danko Meredith for Plaintiff.
Jamie G. Goldstein, Esq. (510-629-4877; jamie@aswtlawyers.com) of Arias Sanguinetti Wang & Torrijos LLP for Intervenor.
Jennifer Dodge, Esq. (310-372-3344; jdodgelaw@jenniferdodgelaw.com) for Defendant, Pacific Gas and Electric Company.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Putative intervenor Erica Martinez Wasdin's motion for leave to intervene is denied on the basis of the Court's prior orders, including its August 4, 2021 order granting defendant P.J. Helicopter's motion to abate and stay Wasdin's separately-filed action, Wasdin v. Pacific Gas and Electric Company, et al., No. CGC-21-591030, and its August 16, 2021 order denying Wasdin's motion to consolidate that action with the instant action. Wasdin cites no statutory or case authority supporting her contention that an heir should be permitted to intervene in a wrongful death action brought by the personal representative. Her position is irreconcilable with the principles that a wrongful death action is "wholly statutory in origin" and that "the right to bring such an action is limited to those persons identified" in the wrongful death statute, Code of Civil Procedure section 377.60. (Stennett v. Miller (2019) 34 Cal.App.5th 284, 290.) Under section 377.60, "[e]ither the decedent's personal representative on behalf of the heirs or the specified heirs . . . may assert the wrongful death claim-but not both." (Adams v. Superior Court (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 71, 77.) Any concerns regarding the adequacy of Plaintiff Ashley King to serve as the personal representative of decedent's estate representing all of decedent's heirs, including Wasdin, must be directed to the Alabama probate court that appointed her.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
